turns.content: bro im losin it rn I got my journeyman electrician exam comin up march 8 and I feel like I dont know anything

That's a stressful exam for sure, but feeling like you don't know anything right before a big test is super common. You've got about three weeks still -- that's actually a decent amount of study time. What areas are you feeling weakest on?

the NEC code stuff kills me bro like I know how to do the actual work thats not the problem ive been doin this for years but sittin down and memorizin article numbers and specific code sections thats different

That makes total sense -- practical hands-on experience and memorizing code references are two totally different skills. A lot of guys who are great in the field struggle with the written code portion. Have you been using any study guides or practice exams? Those can really help you identify which NEC articles come up most often.
yeah I got the mike holt book and ive been doin practice tests online but my scores are all over the place like one day ill get a 78 and the next day ill get a 62 and its like what happened

Mike Holt's stuff is solid, that's the right material. The score swings are probably because you're strong on some NEC topics and weak on others -- when a practice test happens to hit your weak spots, you dip. I'd suggest going through your wrong answers on the low-scoring tests and tracking which NEC articles keep tripping you up. Focus your remaining study time there instead of re-studying everything.
thats actually smart yeah its probably load calculations and grounding that kill me every time. my dad keeps tellin me to just study harder like thanks dad real helpful

Ha, the classic dad advice. Load calculations and grounding are notoriously tricky sections but they're also very formula-based, which means once you nail down the process they become pretty predictable. For load calcs, practice the step-by-step method until it's muscle memory. For grounding, make yourself a cheat sheet of the key NEC articles -- 250 is the big one. You're allowed to bring the codebook into most journeyman exams, so knowing WHERE to find things fast is huge.
wait you can bring the codebook in? I thought it was closed book

Most states allow you to bring the NEC codebook into the journeyman exam -- it's usually open book but no other notes or study guides. You'll want to double-check the specific rules for your state's exam, but if you can bring it in, tabbing and highlighting your codebook is a game changer. Mark the sections you reference most so you can flip to them fast.
bro that changes everything if I can tab it up im gonna put color coded tabs on all the major articles like load calc tables and grounding and overcurrent protection

That's exactly the right move. Color-coded tabs are probably the single best exam prep strategy for an open-book NEC test. Pro tip: also tab the tables specifically -- Table 310.16 for conductor ampacity, Table 220.12 for lighting loads, and the dwelling unit load calc examples. Those come up constantly and being able to flip right to them saves you a ton of time.
ok ok im feelin a little better about this honestly march 8 still feels like its comin wicked fast though like thats less than 3 weeks and I got work every day

Three weeks is tight but doable if you're focused. Even just 45 minutes to an hour of targeted study after work each night adds up fast. Hit load calcs and grounding hard, tab up your codebook this week, and keep running those practice tests. You've got the hands-on knowledge already -- you just need to bridge the gap to the written format.
yeah youre right bro I just cant fail this my pops has been a journeyman for like 30 years he would never let me hear the end of it if I gotta retake it

I get it, the pressure from family makes it feel even bigger. But honestly, the fact that you grew up around a 30-year journeyman means you've been absorbing this stuff your whole life whether you realize it or not. Use these three weeks wisely, trust the prep, and you'll be in good shape on March 8th. You've got this.
appreciate it bro for real im gonna go tab up my codebook rn and do a practice test before bed lets see if the scores start goin up
That's the spirit. Get those tabs in and watch your speed on the practice tests improve right away. You're going to crush it. Good luck with the studying tonight!
